_about: Seeded-violations fixture for pb/tools/lint_registry.py. Valid JSON on purpose (so lint_registry.py parses it), but contains twelve distinct contract violations — one per rule code. CI asserts lint_registry.py --strict flags ALL of them. Do not 'clean up' this file; it is a negative test.
_seeded_violations: R-KEBAB  : component id 'GoodButton' is not kebab-case
R-HEX    : raw hex (#ffffff) in a render body
R-PX     : raw px (44px) in a render body
R-RENDERFN: component 'card' renderFn 'renderCard' breaks the renderCmp<PascalCase> contract
R-SCRIPT : '</script>' literal in a render body (page-killer)
R-DUPID  : id 'card' used by both a global and a local component
R-DTCG-TYPE: token 'brand' has $type 'colour' (not a W3C DTCG type)
R-DANGER : no 'danger' token, but the error runtime needs var(--danger)
R-ORGID  : screen element orgId 'nonexistent' resolves to no component
R-LEVEL  : component 'bad-level' has an invalid atomic level 'gizmo'
R-COMPOSE: non-atom 'bad-level' inlines a raw <button> instead of composing (pbUse)
R-LEVEL-ORDER: molecule 'bad-order' composes the organism 'card' (>= its own level)
